com.mentor.chs.api

Interface IXAbstractPin

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXConnectivityObject, IXObject, IXPin<IXAbstractPinList>, IXWriteableObject
    All Known Subinterfaces:
    IXAbstractCavityObjectsHolder, IXBackshellTermination, IXBlockPin, IXCavity, IXDevicePin, IXFunctionPort, IXInternalPin, IXRefinedCavity, IXSplicePin

    public interface IXAbstractPin
extends IXPin<IXAbstractPinList>, IXConnectivityObject
    Interface for accessing the conductors, signals and owner of a pin.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      java.util.Set<IXAbstractConductor> getConductors()
      Get the conductors connected to this pin.
      java.util.Set<? extends IXAbstractPin> getConnectedPins()
      Get pins connected/mated to this pin
      java.util.Set<IXInternalLink> getInternalLinks()
      Get the internal links terminating at this pin
      IXAbstractPinList getOwner()
      Get the owner pinlist of this pin.
      java.util.Set<IXSignal> getSignals()
      Get the signals connected to this pin.

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.api.IXWriteableObject

        getAttributeSetter

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.api.IXObject

        getAttribute, getAttributes, getBaseID, getID, getParentID, getProperties, getProperty, getSymbol, toHTML, toHTML

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.api.IXConnectivityObject

        getAssembly, getDesign, getInstances, getInstances

    • Method Detail

      • getConductors

        java.util.Set<IXAbstractConductor> getConductors()
        Get the conductors connected to this pin. This will also include signals connected to function port.
        Returns:
        a Set of conductors if any exist, an empty Set otherwise.

      • getSignals

        java.util.Set<IXSignal> getSignals()
        Get the signals connected to this pin. Note that this only has relevance to pins on pinlists in a Capital Integrator design. A pin on a Capital Logic design will not have signals. Use getConductors to get the signals connected to function port.
        Returns:
        a Set of signals if any exist, an empty Set otherwise.

      • getOwner

        IXAbstractPinList getOwner()
        Get the owner pinlist of this pin.
        Specified by:
        getOwner in interface IXPin<IXAbstractPinList>
        Returns:
        the owner pinlist of this pin.

      • getInternalLinks

        java.util.Set<IXInternalLink> getInternalLinks()
        Get the internal links terminating at this pin
        Returns:
        a set of internal links if any exists, an empty set otherwise

      • getConnectedPins

        java.util.Set<? extends IXAbstractPin> getConnectedPins()
        Get pins connected/mated to this pin
        Returns:
        the set of connected pins if any or empty set otherwise. The specific number and type of pins that can be connected to a given pin depend on the pin type and what it is connected to they can also change in the future as design data model gets expanded.
